mnemonics: Remember 'Ricardo' Reads Contracts: Just as Ricardo would read and understand every clause of a legal document, a Ricardian contract is a human-readable text document that is also machine-readable for blockchain execution.
keyPoints: A Ricardian contract is a digital document that defines the terms and conditions of an interaction in a way that is both human-readable and machine-readable.
It cryptographically links a legal agreement to a digital asset or transaction on the blockchain, providing a legal framework for the cryptographic execution.
The core innovation is the ability to have a single document that serves as a legal contract, a user interface, and a protocol for smart contracts.
commonMistakes: Confusing them with smart contracts; while related, a Ricardian contract is the legal document that a smart contract may execute, not the execution code itself.
Assuming they are only for financial transactions; they can be applied to any legal agreement that needs to be represented digitally and executed automatically.
